title: philadelphus microphyllus subsp. pumilus
intro: Philadelphus microphyllus subsp. pumilus, also known as dwarf Mayflower or Dwarf Mock Orange, is a small, deciduous shrub native to Eastern Asia and parts of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthraquinone glycosides content. The health benefits of Philadelphus microphyllus subsp. pumilus include its potential to regulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its usage should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
